title: loranthaceae
intro: The Loranthaceae rank, also known as the Mistletoe family, is a group of flowering plants that are parasitic on other trees. This rank represents a family level in the plant taxonomy classification, consisting of about 73 genera and 900 to 1,000 species worldwide. The Loranthaceae family is predominantly found in tropical and subtropical regions, with some species also occurring in temperate areas. The plants within this family are known for their unique parasitic lifestyle, where they obtain nutrients by connecting to the host plant's vascular system.
